findings.response_summary: Weather info for Korea (April 9), cloudy with rain expected, 11-18°C, sources: weather.go.kr

tRPC_request_flow.description: Initial mutation to create new chat and send user message. Response is NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) with superjson wrapping.
Batch query to fetch chat metadata, all messages, and canvas data after chat creation.
Check user rate limits (9 messages left for anonymous).
Refresh chat data after AI response completed.
Re-check user limits after message exchange.

message_newChat_details.response_format: NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON lines) with superjson serialization

response_body_structure.format: Array of batch results, each line is a JSON object

findings.streaming_mechanism: NOT SSE. Mistral uses tRPC polling (message.get query) rather than SSE/EventStream. The message.newChat mutation creates the chat, then subsequent GET queries fetch the completed response.

analysis.apf_implications: To fake a valid message.newChat response, APF must return NDJSON format with superjson serialization. The response must include valid chatId (UUID), proper batch result structure with nested arrays [0,[{...}]], and meta.values for Date fields. The 896-byte real response provides the exact template needed.
analysis.key_insight: Mistral does NOT use SSE streaming for Le Chat. It uses tRPC batch mutations/queries. The message.newChat POST returns a complete NDJSON batch result immediately, then the frontend polls with message.get to fetch the AI response.
analysis.recommendation: Capture the full 896-byte response body and use it as template for APF fake response, replacing chatId and message content with warning text while preserving the exact NDJSON/superjson structure.
